title: Forces & Motion
objective: Students will explore forces & motion concepts including Pushes & Pulls, Forces Vocabulary, Testing Push & Pull Designs.

steps.content: Have you ever thought about Pushes & Pulls? Let's find out together!
Understand that pushes and pulls are forces that can change the speed or direction of an object's motion, and compare the effects of different strengths and directions
Let's practice together! Describe a push and a pull as types of force that make objects move, speed up, slow down, or change direction
Can you explain that pushing a toy car harder makes it go faster, and pushing it sideways makes it change direction?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Pushes & Pulls. Describe a push and a pull as types of force that make objects move, speed up, slow down, or change direction Compare the effects of a gentle push versus a strong push on the same object
Today we're going to learn about Forces Vocabulary. Get ready for an adventure!

Name and describe forces using precise vocabulary — force, push, pull, twist, stretch, squash, contact force, non-contact force, gravity, weight, friction, air resistance, upthrust — and distinguish between forces that require physical contact and forces that act at a distance
Time to try this out: Correctly name the force acting in at least five given scenarios (e.g. 'friction slows the sledge', 'gravity pulls the apple')
If you slides down a slide and slows near the bottom, can they name the force responsible and spell it correctly?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Forces Vocabulary: Correctly name the force acting in at least five given scenarios (e.g. 'friction slows the sledge', 'gravity pulls the apple') Distinguish between contact forces (friction, upthrust) and non-contact forces (gravity, magnetism) using the correct terms
Today we're going to learn about Testing Push & Pull Designs. Get ready for an adventure!
Analyse data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or pull
Let me show you something cool. Test a simple design (e.g. ramp, launcher) and collect data on whether it meets the goal
If you builds a ramp to make a ball go into a cup, can they test it and figure out what to change if the ball misses?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Testing Push & Pull Designs. Test a simple design (e.g. ramp, launcher) and collect data on whether it meets the goal Analyse results to determine if the design works as intended
